paracetamol.use: Fever and mild to moderate pain (headache, body pain).
paracetamol.dosage: Adults: 500mg–650mg every 4–6 hours if needed (max 3000mg/day unless doctor says).
warnings: Do not exceed maximum daily dose
People with liver disease should consult doctor
food_interactions: Can be taken with or without food.
High-fiber meals may slightly delay absorption.
lifestyle_interactions: Strictly avoid alcohol (increases risk of severe liver damage).
Be cautious when taking other medications containing acetaminophen.
paracetamol.pediatric_caution: Safe in children when strictly dosed by body weight. Avoid adult tablet formulations.
paracetamol.geriatric_caution: Safe for older adults. Monitor total daily dose closely if liver/kidney functions are reduced.

ibuprofen.use: Pain, inflammation, fever (body pain, swelling, toothache).
warnings: Avoid if you have ulcers or gastric bleeding
Avoid in kidney disease (doctor advice needed)
food_interactions: Always take with food or a glass of milk to prevent stomach upset/acidity.
lifestyle_interactions: Avoid alcohol (increases risk of stomach bleeding and ulcers).
Ensure proper hydration, especially in warm weather or during exercise.
ibuprofen.pediatric_caution: Do not use under 6 months of age unless directed by a pediatrician.
ibuprofen.geriatric_caution: High risk of stomach bleeding and kidney strain. Use the lowest effective dose for the shortest duration.

warnings: Consult doctor if pregnant or breastfeeding
food_interactions: Can be taken with or without food.
lifestyle_interactions: Avoid alcohol (can significantly increase drowsiness and impair concentration).
Do not drive or operate machinery if you feel drowsy or dizzy.
cetirizine.pediatric_caution: Safe for children over 2 years of age in liquid form. Dosing must be strictly adjusted by age.
cetirizine.geriatric_caution: Increased risk of drowsiness, dry mouth, and confusion. Consider a lower dose (5mg) to start.

omeprazole.dosage: Adults: 20mg once daily before food (as prescribed).
warnings: Do not use long-term without doctor
May reduce absorption of vitamins/minerals if overused
food_interactions: Must take on an empty stomach, at least 30-60 minutes before breakfast for maximum effectiveness.
lifestyle_interactions: Avoid smoking (smoking reduces the effectiveness of this acid-blocker).
Limit consumption of caffeine, carbonated drinks, and spicy foods as they trigger acidity.
omeprazole.pediatric_caution: Not recommended for children under 1 year of age unless specifically prescribed by a pediatrician.
omeprazole.geriatric_caution: Long-term use increases the risk of bone fractures and vitamin B12 deficiency. Monitor bone density.
